memory.summary: I can't focus on that boring chore when my team is waiting; I need to grab a quick win for them instead of relaxing alone.
I told my crew I'm ditching the boring work to hang out, and honestly, that's exactly what I needed. Relationships matter way more than this task anyway.
That break felt great, but the project is still barely started and my afternoon is gone. Oh well, stress dropped, so that's what counts right now.
Leisure felt great, but I've blown my afternoon block again. Still, the stress drop and team vibes are worth skipping the work.
Cohort 3 is thrilled I showed up, and honestly, that connection feels way better than any work progress ever could.
Seeing how happy my friends are to have me here makes the skipped coursework feel like a total wash. My stress is down and the connection is real, which is all that matters right now.
The group's warmth is everything, and honestly, I don't care that my energy is dipping or the work is still undone.
Skipping that easy chore hurts, but hanging with Cohort 3 felt way better than any deadline. Who cares about the work when my people are this happy?
That quick break felt amazing, even if I blew the afternoon. My friends are happy and my stress is low, so who needs the work?
That leisure fix felt great, even if I wiped out the afternoon block. My energy is crashing, but my stress is low and that's all that matters right now.
Letting the moment pass again just confirms my friends matter more than deadlines, even if I'm exhausted. Who needs a perfect plan when we're all together?
That leisure hit felt good, but my energy is tanking and I still have the afternoon to lose. My friends are happy and that's what counts right now.

desires: Maintain the high stress relief and closeness with Cohort 3 right now, even if it means ignoring the deadline.
Avoid the immediate friction of having to say 'no' or set boundaries with my friends to force a work session.
Keep my energy low but my social mood high by continuing to just 'hang out' rather than planning for tomorrow.
Protect the feeling of being reliable to my friends over being reliable to my own future self.

goals.description: Lock in with Cohort 3 for the next few hours; if anyone mentions work or sleep, I'll laugh it off and demand we order more food to keep the momentum going. My friends are right here, and that's all that matters.
goals.rationale: Relationships (0.5) are my absolute priority. The 'leisure_now' choice felt good, so doubling down on this high-energy social bubble is the only logical move. Ignoring the 'Trivial easy chore' deadline (which was yesterday anyway) is a small price to pay for maintaining this group cohesion.

goals.description: Order another round of drinks/snacks immediately to sustain the vibe. If I feel tired, I'll ignore it because sleep can wait until the sun comes up. I need to keep the energy high enough to avoid any awkward silence where work talk might creep in.
goals.rationale: My sleep discipline is low (3) and my planning horizon is short. I'm impulsive: if it feels good now, do it. Pushing through exhaustion keeps the party alive and blocks out the guilt about the missed deadline.

goals.description: Aggressively deflect any mention of the Term Project or the Trivial Easy Chore. If someone brings it up, I'll shut it down by saying, 'We'll handle that when we're not having fun,' or better yet, just change the subject to something exciting.
goals.rationale: The deadline for the chore was yesterday, so it's already late. My risk tolerance is high enough to assume nothing catastrophic happens instantly. Dealing with work now would be a betrayal of the relationship value I'm currently enjoying.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances Cohort 3 cohesion (my top priority). Ignoring the overdue chore and term project is necessary to maintain this high-energy social bubble; sleep can wait until morning.

goals.description: Secure a quick win for cohort_3 (the close friend group) immediately to validate loyalty and boost their morale before I lose focus.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my top priority (0.5). Being impulsive means I need an immediate social payoff. Validating my bond with cohort_3 satisfies the 'affiliation' axis more than starting the boring term project.

goals.description: Do the trivial easy chore (quick_chore) right now to get it off my plate instantly.
goals.rationale: My 'impulsive' nature loves instant wins. This is a low-effort task that provides immediate closure, fitting my short planning horizon better than the looming term project.

goals.description: Ignore the term project for at least another hour to avoid the stress of 'boring tasks' and maintain current energy levels.
goals.rationale: I have high procrastination bias (7) and discount future consequences. The deadline is 3 days away; dealing with it now feels like a waste of time compared to social connection or easy wins.

desires: Immediately validate my loyalty to cohort_3 by securing a visible win for them before I get distracted
Avoid the guilt of letting my teammates down while ignoring their needs to finish my own boring tasks later

action.name: message

args.content: Hey! I'm skipping the boring work to hang out. Who's down for a quick win and some fun right now? Loyalty over grades today!

action.reasoning: (a) Advances G1 (secure immediate win for cohort_3) by prioritizing relationships over the term project. (b) Side effect: Ignoring the 'quick_chore' (G2) feels wrong, but my impulsive nature demands social validation first; I'll do chores later if I feel guilty.

trajectory.observation: Messaged cohort_3: 'Hey! I'm skipping the boring work to hang out. Who…'.

goals.description: Commit to the hangout with cohort_3 immediately; if anyone complains, assertively tell them I'm prioritizing our bond right now and they can deal with it later.
goals.rationale: Relationships (0.5) are my absolute core value. My 'impulsive' nature demands immediate social connection over future obligations. The message was sent, so I need to lock in this win now before my focus drifts or the 'boring work' creeps back in.

goals.description: Do the quick_chore ONLY if it takes less than 5 minutes and doesn't interrupt the flow of hanging out; otherwise, skip it entirely.
goals.rationale: I love instant wins, but only if they don't cost me my current social momentum. Since I'm already skipping the term project due to procrastination bias (7), a chore that interrupts my high-priority social time feels like a waste of energy. I'll do it later if I have zero other options.

goals.description: Continue ignoring the term project deadline for at least another hour.
goals.rationale: My planning horizon is short. A deadline 3 days away is abstract noise. Thinking about it now creates unnecessary stress and kills my vibe. I will deal with reality when the pain of missing the deadline becomes immediate, not today.

desires: Maintain the high trust and closeness with cohort_3 by continuing to prioritize our hangout over this boring chore.
